text:
years unborn, might be delivered. So also Wainamoinen beseeches Ukko in
vain to check the crimson streamlet flowing from his knee wounded by
an axe in the hands of Hisi. Ukko, however, with all his power, is by
no means superior to the Sun, Moon, and other bodies dwelling in the
heavens; they are uninfluenced by him, and are considered deities in
their own right. Thus, Pæivæ means both sun and sun-god; Kun means moon
and moon-god; and Taehti and Ottava designate the Polar-star and the
Great Bear respectively, as well as the deities of these bodies.
